opp.ais_why:
• Per the 6 Feb 2025 rep note: 'Meeting with Adam to review our AI capabilities. He mentioned RFI is coming in months.' Per the 27 Apr 2026 note: 'Following a 1 year of engagement, RFI finally expected by end of mid of May. Zycus well positioned leading with AI embedded I2O capabilities.' Buyer-side Adam has explicitly asked to review Zycus's AI capabilities ‚Äî this is a buyer-originated AI focus.
• Per the 11 May 2026 note: 'RFI invitation arrive today- the cycle will begin in earnest- ck to reach out to STE for guidance and support to navigate execo.' Per 20 Mar 2026: 'Finalizing the RFI focused on S2C. Competitors Gep, Coupa, iValua, Wday, Jagger. Process: RFI/RFP Sep, Oct/ final decision Dec. STE informed about process and happy to support. Shirley & Wing On invited at AI RT.' Buyer-side Shirley and Wing On invited to an AI Round Table event ‚Äî additional buyer-side AI engagement signal.
• Confidence is High because the buyer has specifically asked to review AI capabilities (Feb 2025), AI-themed RT attendance is invited, and the RFI lands May 2026 with Zycus positioning around 'AI embedded I2O capabilities'. Caveats: Only ML iAnalyze in product scope (no Merlin/Agentic AI/ANA yet), competitive set is heavy (GEP/Coupa/iValua/Workday/Jaggaer), and the RFI itself is just landing ‚Äî no formal AI scoring criteria visible. Score 7 reflects buyer-stated AI focus with the formal cycle just beginning.

items.quote: Contact Roles name a Decision Maker (Wing Yu Onz) but no budget-owning EB/CFO sponsor
no buyer-stated savings, payback, or cost-of-inaction on record
no Avoma calls found for this opp; all engagement is via RFI portal and email

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case (cost savings, cycle-time, headcount, error-rate, payback) has been co-created with Temasek across the engagement. The Next Step history from 2024 through 2026-07-01 records process and AI-capability discussion but no buyer-stated financial impact, and the Feb 2026 Adam Kang meeting was AI-capability focused, not value quantification. The RFI response (2026-06-06) presumably carried a value proposition but no activity records buyer acknowledgement of quantified metrics. In a $325k five-vendor RFI the rival who lands a CFO-grade savings and cost-of-inaction story wins budget buy-in, so quantifying value with procurement before shortlist is the highest-leverage move.

sources: OpportunityContactRole Adam Kang / Wing Yu Onz / Shirley Tan
champion.narrative: No active champion with internal-selling behaviour or economic-buyer access is documented. Adam Kang (Assistant VP, Procurement) is the most engaged buyer contact, coordinating RFI access and sharing process intelligence, but that is coaching, not advocacy. Wing Yu Onz (Decision Maker) and Shirley Tan (VP Procurement) accepted AI Round Table invitations (Next Step 2026-03-20) yet no record shows them pushing back on rivals or introducing a budget sponsor. Converting Adam Kang or Wing Yu Onz into a champion who drives the shortlist internally is a prerequisite before the December decision.

competition.narrative: A five-vendor formal RFI: GEP, Coupa, iValua, Jaggaer and Workday (Next Step 2026-03-20: 'Competitors Gep, Coupa, iValua, Wday, Jagger'). Workday is both a bidder and the buyer's likely ERP, so a stay-with-Workday path is a live threat, underscored by the buyer's request for Workday integration proof and a QIT reference (Task 2026-05-25). GEP is the closest size and scope peer. No buyer statement yet ranks or favours any vendor, so the field is neutral and open; Zycus's wedge is AI-embedded intake-to-orchestration versus bolt-on AI, which must be made concrete in the clarification responses.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer is modernising Source-to-Contract for the Temasek holding group, with emerging interest that hardened into a formal RFI after roughly a year of engagement (Next Step 2026-04-27: 'Following a 1 year of engagement, RFI finally expected'). Buyer-originated AI focus is a confirmed driver: Adam Kang asked to review Zycus's AI capabilities (Next Step 2026-02-06) and Shirley and Wing On were invited to an AI Round Table (Next Step 2026-03-20). The specific operational or financial pain (current tooling gaps, manual effort, compliance risk) has not been articulated in quantified terms, so pain is inferred from the RFI scope rather than confirmed by the buyer.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are early but real: an NDA was executed between Temasek and Zycus on 2026-04-20 (Task 2026-05-11), and the RFI package invokes Temasek's IT Security Standards & Policies for compliance (Task 2026-05-12), signalling an InfoSec review will gate any award. No MSA/SOW, legal redline, or procurement-channel detail is yet visible because the deal sits pre-shortlist. Visibility will come once the buyer names finalists and opens security due diligence; asking procurement for the post-shortlist paper timeline would create it.

sources: OpportunityContactRole Wing Yu Onz Decision Maker
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or budget owner above procurement is identified. Contact Roles name Wing Yu Onz (Director, Procurement) as Decision Maker and PRIMARY, plus VP-level evaluators Shirley Tan, Hui Yeeng Choong and Jiahui Gan, but none is confirmed to control the commercial decision or budget. The Next Step references reaching out to STE (a Temasek-related sponsor) 'to support from back end as a high profile Temasek subsid' (Next Step 2026-05-11), which shows awareness of the need for senior backing but no mapped C-level sponsor. Identifying and reaching the person who owns the S2C budget before the shortlist decision is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, procurement-led competitive process: NDA signed 2026-04-20, RFI invitation 2026-05-11, RFI submitted 2026-06-06, submission under review from 2026-06-15, and a 60-question clarification round issued 2026-07-01 due 2026-07-15. The buyer's own roadmap (Next Step 2026-03-20) is RFI/RFP in Sep-Oct with a final decision in December. Procurement (Adam Kang, Wing Yu Onz) drives the RFx mechanics and portal access; a shortlist gate follows the clarification round. The process is clear and on rails, but Zycus does not yet control it.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is a structured RFI for a 'Procurement Supplier Management initiative' (RFI-PRO-2026-03609) with AI-embedded intake-to-orchestration positioning as Zycus's stated wedge (Next Step 2026-04-27: 'Zycus well positioned leading with AI embedded I2O capabilities'). Confirmed criteria include Workday integration and the buyer's IT Security Standards & Policies compliance, both raised in the RFI (Task 2026-05-12, Task 2026-05-25 QIT and Workday reference request). The 60 clarification questions received 2026-07-01 will further reveal the scoring priorities. Full weighting across the five-vendor field is not yet visible, so criteria are partial, not confirmed.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Formal Evaluation' anchors near 35. Thin signals pull the read to 19.
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

bullets.text: Live next steps with named dates

blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: A five-vendor formal RFI (GEP, Coupa, iValua, Jaggaer, Workday) with no buyer-stated lean yet, so the field is open.

bullets.text: No economic buyer or C-level sponsor mapped above procurement Decision Maker Wing Yu Onz.
Workday is the buyer's ERP and a bidder; a stay-with-Workday path threatens a best-of-breed award.
No quantified ROI or business case co-created; price defensibility unproven in a five-vendor field.
December decision leaves no room to contract by the 18 Dec close date; slip into Q1 2027 is likely.

items: Submit sharp AI-embedded answers to the 60 RFI clarifications by 15 Jul. (by 14 Jul)
Ask Adam Kang who owns the S2C budget and request an intro to that sponsor. (by 17 Jul)
Deliver the Workday integration proof and QIT reference the buyer requested. (by 18 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 19 is carried by commercial strength +0.40 (weight 5). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 51 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 8 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 11 is anchored on the stage win (19), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 51, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 8 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS score 7 (Moderate). Buyer-originated AI interest is real: Adam Kang requested an AI-capabilities review (Feb 2026) and stakeholders were invited to AI Round Table and AI World Tour events, but no formal AI scoring criteria are yet visible in the RFI and Merlin/agentic AI is not in scope.

Amount set to $325k after adjustment from earlier $600k/$310k figures
Buyer returned a 60-question RFI clarification request due 15 Jul

deal_movement.summary: The deal converted from a long dormant relationship into a live formal RFI in 2026: qualified 2026-04-24, moved to Formal Evaluation 2026-05-11, RFI submitted 2026-06-06, and a 60-question clarification round arrived 2026-07-01. Engagement is two-way and current as of 2026-07-01.

Adam Kang coordinates the RFI and shares process intelligence (coaching), and Wing Yu Onz is the passive Decision Maker; neither is documented introducing a budget sponsor, pushing back on rivals, or driving Zycus internally. In a five-vendor RFI heading to a December decision, developing a champion who owns the shortlist narrative is the priority.

items: Buyer confirmed RFI responses under review (Task 2026-06-15).
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and warming, not cooling: the buyer issued a 60-question RFI clarification round on 2026-07-01 and confirmed active review on 2026-06-15.

north_star_verdict.math: Deal created 2024-07-25, 708 days old. Moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation 2026-05-11 when the RFI invitation landed. RFI submitted 2026-06-06; buyer returned 60 clarification questions 2026-07-01 due 2026-07-15, so engagement is live 2 days ago, not silent. Close 2026-12-18 is 168 days out. Buyer-stated process (Next Step 2026-03-20): RFI/RFP Sep-Oct, final decision Dec. To close by 2026-12-18 the whole chain (shortlist, RFP, vendor selection, negotiation, InfoSec, contracting) must complete after a Dec decision, which is not feasible. The date will slip into Q1 2027 even if Zycus wins.
evidence: Buyer engagement is LIVE: Temasek returned a 60-question RFI clarification request on 2026-07-01 due 2026-07-15 (Task 2026-07-01, Next Step 2026-07-01), and confirmed on 2026-06-15 they were reviewing Zycus's RFI responses.
RFI submitted on time 2026-06-06 after the invitation arrived 2026-05-11 (Next Step); NDA signed 2026-04-20 (Task 2026-05-11).
Buyer-stated timeline (Next Step 2026-03-20): 'Process: RFI/RFP Sep, Oct/ final decision Dec.' A December decision leaves no room to contract by 2026-12-18.

Forecast Best Case is indefensible: five-vendor RFI (GEP, Coupa, iValua, Jaggaer, Workday per Next Step 2026-03-20), economic buyer unmapped above Decision Maker Wing Yu Onz, no active champion, no quantified ROI.
Scope clarified 2026-05-25 to the Temasek HOLDING group only, not the ~300-company portfolio (Task 2026-05-25), which bounds deal size and C-level visibility.
Owner is VP APAC Carl Kimball, so the account is already senior-owned; no manager escalation is warranted.
north_star_verdict.headline: Live formal RFI: buyer returned 60 clarification questions on 1 Jul (due 15 Jul), so the deal is engaged, but the 18 Dec close is not credible with shortlist, RFP, selection, negotiation and contracting all still ahead in a five-vendor race.

flags: Map and reach the economic buyer / budget owner above Decision Maker Wing Yu Onz before the shortlist gate; use STE backing to get there.
Neutralise Workday incumbency by delivering a concrete Workday-integration proof and QIT reference the buyer asked for on 25 May.
Convert Adam Kang or Wing Yu Onz from process coach into an active champion who drives the shortlist decision internally.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for a live five-vendor RFI where Zycus is engaged but does not yet own the process.

competitive_position.summary: A five-vendor formal RFI (GEP, Coupa, iValua, Jaggaer, Workday) with no buyer-stated lean yet, so the field is open. GEP is the strongest current threat as the closest size and scope peer in the same RFI; Workday is the incumbent-ERP threat because the buyer asked for Workday integration proof and a QIT reference on 2026-05-25, raising a stay-with-Workday risk. Coupa, iValua and Jaggaer are active RFI participants without differentiated buyer signal. Zycus's wedge is AI-embedded intake-to-orchestration versus bolt-on AI.

competitors.how_we_win: Position embedded Merlin AI across sourcing and analytics against GEP's services-led model; make AI the RFI-response centrepiece.

competitors.quote: require QIT and reference request for Workday, more clarifications
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver a strong Workday-integration reference (QIT) to neutralise the stay-with-ERP path and prove best-of-breed S2C depth.

competitors.quote: Competitors Gep, Coupa, iValua, Wday, Jagger
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus's embedded AI I2O with Coupa's spend-led positioning on the S2C-specific criteria.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: Competitors Gep, Coupa, iValua, Wday, Jagger
competitors.how_we_win: Lead on time-to-value and embedded AI versus iValua's configuration-heavy platform.
